channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption
item.description: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous natural disasters.
item.content:encoded:
Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous natural disasters.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
item.description: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."
item.content:encoded:
Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
item.description: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
item.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

(Image credit: Dmitry Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: East Africa redefines marathon limits as Sabastian Sawe leads historic charge
item.description: East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.
item.content:encoded:
East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.

(Image credit: Alberto Pezzali)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Igunza

item.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
item.description: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.
item.content:encoded:
Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

(Image credit: Mahmoud Illean)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
item.description: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.content:encoded: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Chernobyl survivors have paid the ultimate price in the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: In a Kyiv apartment building housing the families of Chernobyl workers, a wartime tragedy strikes three friends preparing to mark 40 years since the nuclear accident.
item.content:encoded: In a Kyiv apartment building housing the families of Chernobyl workers, a wartime tragedy strikes three friends preparing to mark 40 years since the nuclear accident.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
item.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.
item.content:encoded:
A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

(Image credit: Meysam Mirzadeh)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
